content.title: Philosophy & Inspirations
content.description:
Hobson draws from several foundational ideas in interactive computing, personal information management, and live programming environments. Understanding these influences helps explain why the system works the way it does.

---

## Conway's Humane Dozen

Mel Conway's [Humane Dozen](https://vimeo.com/44681771) describes twelve attributes that make interactive applications feel natural and empowering. Hobson aspires to embody all of them.

### Hands-On Attributes

| Attribute | Meaning | How Hobson Achieves It |
|-----------|---------|------------------------|
| **Immediate** | Changes visible instantly | Saving an item re-renders it immediately |
| **Continuous** | Small steps yield predictable results | Items are granular; edits are atomic |
| **Interactive** | Each action suggests the next | Links and context menus reveal pathways |
| **Transparent** | The tool disappears; you touch the material | Everything is an item; no hidden layers |
| **Inspectable** | Any part can be examined | REPL, JSON view, debug mode |
| **Modifiable** | Change work mid-stream | Edit code items while system runs |
| **Robust** | Actions are reversible, system doesn't break | Safe mode, type validation, error capture |

### Single-Mode Attributes

| Attribute | Meaning | How Hobson Achieves It |
|-----------|---------|------------------------|
| **Unified** | Source and runtime are the same | Code items *are* the running code |
| **Self-Revealing** | Interface presents choices | Context menus, type pickers, search |
| **Symmetrical** | Tool and application are peers | Hobson can render and edit itself |
| **Always On** | No start/stop cycle | Browser-based, IndexedDB persistence |
| **Alive with Data** | See data flow in real time | Partial re-render, event system |

---

## Smalltalk's Image-Based Persistence

[Smalltalk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smalltalk) pioneered the idea that the entire development environment--objects, classes, methods, and state--lives in a persistent "image" rather than external files. When you close Smalltalk, you save the image; when you reopen it, everything is exactly as you left it.

Hobson adapts this concept:

- **IndexedDB as image**: All items persist in the browser's IndexedDB
- **No build step**: Code items are evaluated directly from storage
- **Continuity**: Close the tab, reopen it, and your workspace remains
- **Hot reload**: Edit a kernel module, reload, and changes take effect

Unlike Smalltalk, Hobson stores code as text (ES modules) rather than bytecode, enabling inspection and editing with standard tools.

---

## Lisp's REPL-First Development

[Lisp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lisp_(programming_language)) cultures emphasize the REPL (Read-Eval-Print Loop) as the primary interface for development. You write expressions, evaluate them immediately, see results, and iterate. The distinction between "writing code" and "running code" dissolves.

Hobson's REPL (accessed via `Esc` or `Ctrl+\`):

- Provides full access to the kernel API
- Allows creating, querying, and modifying items directly
- Supports multi-line code with history
- Enables testing code before persisting it as a code item

The system can be operated entirely through the REPL if desired--the graphical interface is a convenience, not a requirement.

---

## Obenauer's Itemized OS

[Alexander Obenauer's Lab Notes](https://alexanderobenauer.com/labnotes/) explore what he calls an "itemized" approach to personal computing. Key ideas:

### Universal Data Portability

Any item can be moved anywhere in the system. There are no application silos--a note can become a child of a project, a search result, or a workspace item.

### Universal Reference Containers

Any item can contain references to any other items. This enables:
- Workspaces (containers of mixed item types)
- Bidirectional linking (via tags or explicit references)
- Arbitrary hierarchies without schema constraints

### Separation of Services and Views

Data storage is independent of presentation. The same item can be rendered as:
- A spatial window
- A list entry
- A card
- Raw JSON

Views are items too--users can modify or create them.

### Graph OS

Rather than folders and files, everything forms a knowledge graph. Items connect through:
- Parent-child relationships (attachments array)
- Type relationships (type field)
- Tags (tags array)
- Transclusion (item:// links)

---

## Obsidian-Style Linking

[Obsidian](https://obsidian.md) popularized bidirectional linking in note-taking. Hobson adapts this with `item://` URLs:

### Navigation Links

```markdown
[See the kernel](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000)
```

Clicking opens the linked item (as sibling window in spatial view, or navigation otherwise).

### Full Transclusion

```markdown
![Kernel Core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000)
```

Embeds the entire item inline, rendered with its own view.

### Partial Transclusion

```markdown
![](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#description)
![](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=SEED_IDS)
![](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#code?lines=10-20)
```

Embeds specific fields, named regions, or line ranges.

### Query Blocks

````markdown
```query
const modules = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.KERNEL_MODULE });
return modules.map(m => `- ${m.name}`).join('\n');
```
````

Dynamic content generated at render time.

---

## The Synthesis

Hobson synthesizes these influences into a coherent system:

| Influence | Contribution |
|-----------|-------------|
| Conway | UX principles: immediacy, inspectability, unity |
| Smalltalk | Persistence model: everything in the image |
| Lisp | Interaction model: REPL-first, code as data |
| Obenauer | Data model: items, types, universal containers |
| Obsidian | Linking model: bidirectional, transclusion, queries |

The result is a system where:
- You can inspect and modify anything
- Changes take effect immediately
- Navigation and structure emerge from usage
- The tool and the content are made of the same material
